research_question: After round 2 selected Alpha158 CORR30 as the only passing head-extraction standalone candidate, does it still pass under a stricter confirmatory recheck that freezes the same trainval-only artifacts and tightens only the promotion rule through subperiod stability, cost-stress, low-liquidity, and TopK-perturbation gates?

decision_rule.boolean_expression: (validation_annual_relative_return_delta > 0.10) AND (validation_relative_ir_delta > 0.50) AND (validation_max_drawdown_delta >= 0.10) AND (validation_avg_turnover_daily_delta <= 0.02) AND (candidate_avg_invested_weight >= 0.18) AND (validation_h1_annual_relative_return_delta > 0) AND (validation_h1_relative_ir_delta > 0) AND (validation_h2_annual_relative_return_delta > 0) AND (validation_h2_relative_ir_delta > 0) AND (cost_stress_annual_relative_return_delta > 0) AND (low_liquidity_weight_share_delta <= 0) AND (topk8_annual_relative_return_delta > 0) AND (topk12_annual_relative_return_delta > 0)
decision_rule.interpretation: The candidate must not only beat v18 in aggregate validation, but also preserve that advantage across both validation subperiods, under cost stress, under TopK perturbation, and on low-liquidity exposure.

disallowed_changes: Do not rebuild or retune the candidate strategy in this round.
Do not swap reference candidate, snapshot, or contract.
Do not change TopK, cost stress assumptions, or subperiod boundaries after registration.
Do not add any new candidate or reopen the shortlist.
notes: This round narrows the scope to the single passing round-2 candidate only.
The only changed dimension is promotion rule strictness; no strategy artifact is changed or rebuilt.
The strict gate is intentionally kept identical to the earlier VSUMD60 strict recheck so that outcomes remain comparable.
